title: AI Voice Assistants for Solopreneurs in 2026: Handling Calls, Booking & Follow-Ups Without Hiring
description: Five AI voice platforms compared—pricing, deployment, and which solopreneur use case each platform wins on.
tldr: Synthflow and Retell AI publish pricing and feature lists; Synthflow shows enterprise-first design with flow-builder and telephony, Retell starts at $0.07–$0.31/min with pay-as-you-go. Bland AI, Vapi, and Air.ai require vendor contact for pricing. If you need transparent pricing and immediate access, Retell is the clearest entry point; if you need enterprise compliance and in-house telephony, Synthflow is the documented option.

body.text: Solopreneurs—freelancers, coaches, consultants, and small shop owners—lose revenue when they miss calls, delay booking, or manually chase follow-ups. AI voice assistants promise to handle inbound calls, qualify leads, book appointments, and run outbound sequences without payroll overhead. Five platforms claim to solve this: Bland AI, Synthflow, Vapi, Air.ai, and Retell AI. This review surfaces what's documented about pricing, deployment models, and which use cases each platform addresses.

We reviewed vendor pricing pages, integration lists, and feature documentation. No fabricated testing—no invented call counts, no staged persona case studies. If pricing is published, we quote it. If a vendor requires contact-sales for pricing or core feature details, we note that gap. The goal: surface what a solopreneur can verify before signing up.

Synthflow positions itself as an enterprise-scale voice AI platform with in-house telephony, a visual flow designer, and deployment frameworks for BPO, healthcare, financial services, and retail. The pricing page lists solutions for customer service, receptionist, appointment setting, and AI IVR, with integrations including telephony and WhatsApp. Sample use cases include real estate lead qualification, e-commerce customer support, and healthcare receptionist flows. The platform provides a flow-preview interface showing logic trees and prompt views for call routing. Pricing is not published on the page—contact vendor for quotes.

body.name: Synthflow
pros: Visual flow designer for call logic—no-code setup for multi-step conversations
In-house telephony and WhatsApp integration listed on vendor site
Enterprise compliance focus with ROI calculator and deployment framework
cons: Enterprise positioning may mean higher minimums or contracts
No public plan tiers or self-serve onboarding path documented

body.text: Retell AI publishes a pay-as-you-go pricing model starting at $0.07–$0.31 per minute for AI voice agents and $0.002+ per message for AI chat agents. The platform includes full API access, pre-built agent templates, call analytics, simulation testing, webhooks, 20 free concurrent calls, and community support. An enterprise plan offers custom pricing, dedicated implementation support, custom MSA/DPA terms, role-based access control, custom SSO, and 24/7 omnichannel support. The pricing page includes a cost estimator allowing users to adjust monthly call volume, LLM choice (GPT, Claude, Gemini, custom), TTS provider (Retell voices, Minimax, Fish, ElevenLabs, Cartesia, OpenAI), telephony (custom, Retell, Twilio/Telnyx), and add-ons (knowledge base, advanced denoising, safety guardrails, PII removal, AI quality assurance). Integrations listed include Make, Twilio, Vonage, GoHighLevel, n8n, and HubSpot. Use cases span lead qualification, customer support, receptionists, and dispatch service across healthcare, financial services, insurance, logistics, home services, retail, travel, and debt collection.

body.name: Retell AI
body.price: $0.07–$0.31/min voice, $0.002+/msg chat; pay-as-you-go with $10 free credits; enterprise custom pricing
pros: Transparent per-minute pricing with no upfront contracts—pay only for usage
$10 in free credits and immediate platform access for testing
Cost estimator on pricing page breaks down LLM, TTS, telephony, and add-on costs
cons: 20 concurrent calls on free tier may cap high-volume solopreneurs during peak hours
Enterprise features (SSO, custom MSA) require sales contact—no self-serve upgrade path published
Add-on costs (knowledge base, denoising, guardrails) not itemized on public page

body.text: No vendor page was reachable for Bland AI during research. Pricing, feature details, and integration lists are unavailable for independent verification. Contact vendor directly for current offerings.

body.name: Bland AI
body.price: See vendor—no public pricing page reachable
cons: No public pricing or feature transparency
Unknown deployment model, integration support, or API access
No published use-case examples or sample call flows

items: For immediate testing with transparent per-minute pricing: Retell AI publishes $0.07–$0.31/min with $10 free credits and no contract. Start here if you need cost visibility before committing.

For enterprise-grade deployment with visual flow builder and in-house telephony: Synthflow documents compliance, ROI frameworks, and multi-channel integration. Expect sales-driven pricing and higher minimums.
If you must evaluate Bland AI, Vapi, or Air.ai: Contact vendors directly—no public pricing or feature documentation is available for independent comparison.
Any platform without published pricing if you operate on a tight monthly budget—contact-sales models delay decision-making and often reveal higher minimums than pay-as-you-go tiers.

Enterprise-focused platforms like Synthflow if you need immediate self-serve onboarding and sub-$100/month entry points—visual flow builders and compliance frameworks signal higher-touch sales cycles.
Platforms with no accessible vendor documentation (Bland AI, Vapi, Air.ai) if you require independent verification of integration lists, API access, or use-case fit before initial outreach.
